reviews.author: Greg Bolden
reviews.text: After walking half a mile on the Lee-White Beltline, we were surprised to find a plant based ice cream stand. The music and atmosphere compliments the pleasant vibes.  My lady and I purchased a scoop of pineapple and one watermelon which were mouthwatering. A wonderful touch to the summer evening ✨️ We will be back!

reviews.author: Bernette Fondong
reviews.text: I came here and they were so nice! The man who served me also told me about partnering with the youth in ATL! They give back to the community and I really loved hearing everything they do around the city. Service was great, good price, the italian ice flavors were delicious, and it’s for a good cause. I also got a stamp card! I would totally recommend coming here.

reviews.author: Nichelle Veasley
reviews.text: I couldn’t wait to dig in before I posted. Glaciers has got to be the best Italian ice I’ve ever tasted. The customer service was terrific, and the environment was welcoming. I also liked that they hire young black men from the community and offer more than just Italian ice. Glaciers take a personal interest in the community.

reviews.author: Tishonna Cherry
reviews.text: Matt was so nice he let me sample some flavors for my first visit! The inside of the restaurant is very beautiful and prices are very reasonable. Summerhill lemonade was so good! 10/10

reviews.author: A Beckett
reviews.text: My kids and I stop by often for the Italian ice, ice cream, and water. They have great customer service and we have a wonderful experience every time. They’re always patient and make us feel appreciated but we truly appreciate them and the message they convey to young people. Plus they have children’s books galore! 💯💯💯

reviews.author: Felicity Muffin
reviews.text:
Came here with a friend while we were in the area to check out Kroger. Webwere greeted warmly upon arrival, the place was clean, bright, and looked new which helped the place look inviting.

Their menu selection is VAST! I'm talking from wings, fries, to east asian food items like spring rolls,
noodles( fried and soup wise),  all present which was the first time I experienced a restaurant like that that was not a buffet( I'm from the east coast). I liked it!

My friend ordered lemon pepper (dry) with shrimp fried rice. I bought lemon pepper dry with french fries and my goodness th portions were plentiful for the price we paid which was such a plus in our book!
.we loved the lemon pepper as they were seasoned to perfection, yum! Friend said the fried rice was bland as it had little to no seasoning. The fries were good and crisp and came with some sort of sauce which I think is their homemade house sauce which taste like a mixture of Siracha, mayo and something else maybe mustard to give it a tang.

All in all my friend and I would and WILL continue to patron this place! Their wings got us sold. Get you some!

reviews.author: SJ
reviews.text: I ordered through Doordash, had to call to make a request and the young lady who answered the phone was so kind and respectful. My food came quickly and was still a good temperature. The wings were fried perfectly and were good. I wasn’t too excited about the fries, but that’s because I don’t care for those type of fries in general; there wasn’t an actual issue with them. Will def be ordering from here again and trying some of their other menu items.

reviews.author: Aaron Murphy
reviews.text: Bruh, it is so hard to find a good Gyro and I was very pleasantly surprised when I had theirs. I thought I may have been gambling but the lamb was perfect and the sauce was almost perfect. Hush puppies were on point too. Can't wait to try more of their menu.

reviews.author: Otis Lee
reviews.text: Very pleasant experience on my visit to I love wings random visit just stop by, modern look inside comfortable seating and lots of choices on the menu. I ordered a Philly cheese steak with a side of fried rice and a drink it cost me around $12 in total. I wasn't disappointed plan to visit again if I'm ever in the area....😋

reviews.author: kappanu xi
reviews.text: The wings was good but the shrimp fried rice was bland with no seasoning at all and the shrimp tasted old.  The I Love Wings in Lawrenceville Ga is the bomb. I should have traveled all the way there. Other than that the service and the atmosphere was good.

reviews.author: Kiran Kumar
reviews.text: Great experience at motel 6. Front desk staff were incredibly welcoming and made check-in quick and easy and made me feel right at home. The room was clean and comfortable for the price. Would definitely stay here again

reviews.author: Jason
reviews.text: I recommend that Motel 6 take several steps to improve the guest experience, including thoroughly cleaning rooms, checking behind doors for any items left by previous guests, and repainting tubs where needed. Additionally, the property should ensure that all non working vehicles are towed, trash is regularly removed especially from the front of the property and stairwells are kept clean. Installing a working ice machine and and it should be common courtesy to offer fresh towels to guests staying multiple nights, such as three or more. I also requested a king room with a refrigerator and did not receive one; honoring simple room preferences, especially for My6 members, would reflect better service and appreciation for loyal customers.

reviews.author: Darius Harris
reviews.text: I appreciate this motel because, when I needed help. The representatives at the front desk assisted me as soon as possible. They got me in my room with no problem. I love the customer service the representatives give when they speak to you…..Keep Up the great work!!!!!!!!!!!!

reviews.author: Darius Harris (Draya Grey)
reviews.text: I love the customer service the representatives gives at the front desk. Every time I had a problem. They handled every issue with no problem….Thank you and Keep up the good work

reviews.author: LilJessee Shockley
reviews.text:
Horrible Place To Stay. It's Not Very Clean Or Quiet. And There's People Hanging Around Outside All Night Long. I Didn't Really Feel Very Safe...Like AT ALL!!
TIP: Keep Windows And Doors Locked At All Times.

reviews.text: We use Ira Lane's coffee truck for work events every holiday season and it is always a winner! The drinks are delicious, with a  great variety (even for non coffee drinkers). Taneisha and her team are always a joy to work with!

reviews.text: Very polite and courteous!!! 1st time stopping at Ira Lane coffee! I’ve drove by their location almost daily! I ordered a turkey bacon sandwich, it was GREAT!! I will be stopping here more often so I can try out other food they have!!⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

reviews.author: Lisa Walker
reviews.text: So many Great American Cookie Companies have closed throughout metro Atlanta over the years. I’m so glad my neighborhood, Greenbriar Mall location is steadfast and I appreciate the consistent, friendly customer service. I miss the previous owner, Sam, but I really like the new (well not so new) ownership. Thank you for serving our community!

reviews.author: Admin User
reviews.text: Just picked up some cookies from the Great American Cookie Company at Greenbriar Mall, and everything was PERFECT! Star took care of us with amazing customer service — super friendly, fast, and made sure our order was just right. The cookies were fresh, warm, and delicious! Definitely recommend stopping by if you’re in the area. Thanks again, Star! 🍪👏🏾

reviews.author: Denise Robinson
reviews.text: I had an amazing experience at the Great American Cookie Company at Greenbriar Mall! Star and her team were absolutely exceptional — friendly, professional, and on point with everything. The customer service was top-tier from start to finish, and the cookies were fresh, delicious, and just how I like them. It’s always a plus when the team behind the counter goes above and beyond to make your visit special. I’ll definitely be back soon — keep up the great work, Star!

reviews.author: Brit Royster
reviews.text: Great customer service experience. Awesome cookie cake! We were a day late picking up our cookie cake and the staff went out of their way to make a fresh one from scratch. 10/10 recommend! Thank you to the Great American Cookie Staff at the Greenbriar location!

reviews.author: Tess Goins
reviews.text: The great American Cookie inside Greenbrier mall has the worst customer service that I’ve ever experienced. The girl cashier was on her phone when we walked up and didn’t even look at us, then she proceeded to say “ what kinda of cookies you want” while still on her phone. Then she put the bag of cookies down on the counter instead of handing them to my fiancé and walked in the back. We that she was going to get the other cookies. When she came bk she said they ran out she forgot to tell us, and she placed the change on the counter instead of putting it inside his hand. One of my biggest pet peeves. Just completely rude and disrespectful

reviews.author: Phone Home
reviews.text: Overall great food and chef, but we left with a little bit of a bad feeling after getting the bill - enough so, that we won't recommend to others.  We were ready for 150 pp cost, and cost of extras (drinks and extra sushi).  What was upsetting was them offering a different beer that "wasn't on the menu yet" then being charged $20 (when every other beer was around 10 bucks).  And them pushing a whiskey on to my wife (suggesting a taste after my wife mentioned the pretty bottle and liking Japanese whiskey when walking by the display) then charging $34 for a single serving.  Again this was off menu, not something we actively asked for, and not in line with the rest of their similar menu items. Just felt skeezy the way it happened.  If items aren't comparably priced then say so.  Even the water was annoying, we just wanted tap ... But okay "still" for 9 bucks a bottle. They even popped open the second bottle without checking or asking if we wanted another.  This was like Switzerland, just in Atlanta. Based on the constant upsell and pushing off high priced items I just can't recommend Neko when there are other great sushi omakasi spots in Atlanta that don't behave this way with their customers and provide amazing sushi.  Heck even high end sushi in DC is better and more adorable.

reviews.author: Ethan McCormick
reviews.text: My wife and I dined here for our anniversary and the experience was great. Chef Johan Lestari did an amazing job preparing the food and informing us about everything we saw and ate. The dinning staff was very attentive and well prepared for our every need.

reviews.author: Richard Scalesse
reviews.text: Amazing experience — fresh, creative dishes and a great vibe. Highly recommend

reviews.author: Myara Beals
reviews.text: THE BEST FOOD I HAVE EVER HAD IN MY LIFE! I loved every moment at this restaurant. Again, the food was absolutely amazing, but the staff were top notch. The entire experience from the moment you step in is incredible!

reviews.author: Alice Shen
reviews.text:
Came to try this restaurant and as always, was so delighted! Set at a very approachable price point for delicious omakase, easy to find, nice ambiance, and very friendly chef and staff :)
Every bite was super yummy and it was crazy to see the attention to detail that went into it all. Everything was fresh, the rice was delicious, and I left very happy and excited for next time!

reviews.author: Brandon Adkins
reviews.text:
I wish I could give this McDonald’s zero stars. Walking in was already a bad idea. The managers and team were completely disrespectful, cursing loudly and being verbally abusive right in front of customers. I wish I had the camera footage from 3:25am-3:54am They all had terrible attitudes, and a few even walked off in the middle of a shift while people were still waiting to be served.

The manager vanished. She was frustrated, yelling , she treated the boy in the window so bad and talked down on him. It didn’t make no sense. She had NO LEADERSHIP or control of the shift. Extremely verbally abusive. She didn’t think to help him because he may have been slower, but there was no teamwork, no accountability, and no cohesiveness.

The restaurant itself was disgusting—dirty floors, ice buckets thrown on the ground, mop buckets with dirty water at the front where they serving food, trash everywhere. The whole shift was pure chaos, nothing but mess and drama. To make things worse, I watched them hand out free food and drinks to their friends, yet I’m still sitting in the lobby waiting for my order. I spent the better end of an hour in that restaurant. The cashier literally called her friends on the phone while I was ordering and made me wait. Then instead of letting me finish ordering just choose to interact with guest about what her friend was saying on the phone.

No one ever apologized. You could literally see the cashier try to argue me on camera because my whole order was wrong.

I ordered a McDouble (no pickle, no onion, well done) meal deal and a snack wrap. I received a mcchicken xtra mayo. I walk back to this McDonald’s for them to fix the order and it’s still wrong a second time!

This is by far the worst McDonald’s experience I’ve ever had. Unprofessional, unsanitary, and flat-out disrespectful. Corporate needs to step in immediately, because this location is out of control. I could come run a better shift than that overnight for FREE!

reviews.author: Trez Trigga
reviews.text: Every time I come in, it’s always a bad experience first off. My niece and I were standing at the counter for a whole 10 minutes before they decided to take our order and then the light skin cheek proceeds to walk around with a nasty look on her face like Someone did something to her. and there was this black family. They were dining in, and she had to take their food to their table. She walked with the food with a nasty attitude and that really bothered me even though I had nothing to do with that once I got my food, the tables work a complete mess like no one came out and wipe the table I seen people laying down napkins on the table just to eat their food I would have taken more pictures of the employees, but I didn’t want to start any commotion, but just know I will never come back to the McDonald’s on Metropolitan

reviews.author: K E
reviews.text:
This is the 3rd day in a row
I’ve gotten watered down oatmeal
No one except 1 person speaks English when I asked for the Hispanic bi-lingual manager she prints a receipt and walks away. Meanwhile lying about the amount of water that is supposed to be in oatmeal. This is a very simple job even a robot can do. I’ve been coming to this McDonald’s for months only getting oatmeal. For the last 3 days the manager has allowed improper things to go on at the breakfast time. She needs to be replaced immediately. She is rude and unprofessional. So as I’m sitting down making a complaint about her she sends a young black outside to ask me to move. I told him to send her outside. What’s the problem. I’m making a formal complaint on her and she refused to give her name. The absolute worst manager. She then sends another non-manager outside. I asked for managers not cashiers. You suck

reviews.author: Ernecia Beasley
reviews.text: First time in a long time this location was please , Kassy who was expediting in drive thru was amazing and also the cashier with the green highlights. Extremely mature and high work ethic ! Any other day or time of the week this location sucks but today I’ll give it to them they did amazing.

reviews.author: LaMia Michele
reviews.text: The service here is always excellent. However the food can be a hit or miss. This is the 2nd time I had a not so pleasant experience. Who doesn’t love McDonald's fries? Nice, hot, right amount of salt...and only to get to the end and find hair stuck to one. 🤢 Another occasion I ordered a Hi C orange and there was mold stuck to the ice. Now granted this is the hood so the atmosphere fits the location but the employees are always professional, polite and try their best to accommodate the customers with no attitudes or kickback. Their professionalism is always excellent.

reviews.author: Shaun Tinsley
reviews.text: Had the special (stewed beef with rice & beans, salad and plantains). Also ordered a sorrel. Everything was great and seasoned well. Salad was crisp. Sorrell was super flavorful. Conveniently located on Peachtree Pl in Midtown.

reviews.author: Jaheem Moorer
reviews.text: 10/10 Wouldn’t recommend. Food was so good my girlfriend is now talking about going vegan permanently expecting it to taste like this all the time. My favorite was the everything on the menu. Delicious food! Will definitely be back! 🫡❤️

reviews.author: Jeffrey Darby
reviews.text:
La Jefa Empanadas is hands down the best! Each of their empanadas is cooked perfectly with delicious fillings! We love each and everyone of the different types. There is something for everyone from savory to sweet with different fillings made of vegan chicken, beef, veggies, jams, and sweet plantains. In addition to the empanadas, La Jefa has created delicious and unique sauces to dip into.
Ordering is easy, too! They have a kiosk to order from that is very self-explanatory.
The overall experience is an 11/10!
Be sure and visit their food truck or their brick and mortar location.

reviews.author: Daniel Castillo
reviews.text:
We were excited to see Afro-Latino/Caribbean cuisine featured at a vegan festival in Atlanta, especially at a vegan event where diverse flavors are always a treat. Their food truck had the longest line, a sure sign that something good was cooking. And let’s be honest—who waits in the longest line at a food festival unless the food is worth it?

We stood in line with high hopes that the wait would lead to a meal so delicious it would make us forget the time spent. Thankfully, the food delivered. We ordered every kind of empanada they offered, and each one was flavorful—ranging from savory to sweet. The empanadas were perfectly filling, with a light, crispy crust that wasn’t too thick. The balance was spot on, allowing the filling to shine. It was clear that they emphasized the flavor and texture of the protein in each bite. The empanadas were definitely a good call.

We also ordered the rice and beans. They leaned more towards a Southern U.S. style rather than the traditional Afro-Latino/Caribbean flavors you might expect. While it fit the geographical context of the food truck, it felt like a missed opportunity to showcase the unique island-inspired flavors that could have elevated the dish.

One small critique: two of the empanadas with different names (primarily filled with protein and cheese) tasted almost identical. The only noticeable difference was the color of the protein, as the flavors were nearly indistinguishable.

Overall, the food was great, and if you're wondering whether the wait is worth it—yes, it is. Join the line!

reviews.author: Tianna Walters
reviews.text: The beef empanadas are amazing, Johnny cakes with the powder sugar and guava was crispy/ sweet; loved it. The bean and rice stood no chance. I’ll be back!!

reviews.text: Okay, so the girl that did our order was excellent.I don't know her name, but we ordered two honey hot lemon pepper combos with chicken fried rice.The cook gave us two different wing flavors and when we called the manager to fix it, he said he will not give us any compensation for having.Come back after we already drove home.And they did not show us our order in the store to look at it.He was very disrespectful to us.Do not go here.The only one who was good was the girl that took the order. Someone give her a better job.

reviews.text: Went in yesterday at 9 pm, to get dinner and everything was made fresh. Only downside is if you get chicken that it’s going to be cubed.  (a bit off putting on a Philly cheese) Outside of that the food was immaculate, the area is not the best so be on your p’s & q’s. Also they charge .92 cent if you use card so that’s a down side as well. (6 piece combo not pictured)

reviews.author: Minnie Brown
reviews.text: The owner is extremely nasty my food order was wrong I came back the owner wad very rude very aggressive will not be returning

reviews.text: This spot has gotten SOOOOO much better recently in ALL ways. I actually look forward to ordering their food  these days. It used to be a chore to simply place a call in order, being you always had to order & wait all over again for the food to be cooked. & Then half the time it wasn't even correct.

reviews.text: The food quality is always A1 - a real ATLien

reviews.author: Kara Lee
reviews.text: Finally got to try this today. I had MATCHA and it was made well. I added oat milk (extra $1) the exact order was GA400 Matcha zone 3. The creativity in the names gives a nice touch. I love matcha so I can confidently say they get it right. I’d recommend going here. I’ll be back, it’s close to work and I was in and out pretty quickly. This would be a good spot for a first date. They have games available, the shop is very clean and I love the decor.

reviews.author: Adarin Freeman
reviews.text: 404 Coffee Has The Best COFFEE And Breakfast!!! The ATLANTA And Cozy Atmosphere Makes You Feel Right At Home. The Prices Are Reasonable, Food Is Delicious And The Staff Are Polite. That Place Brings Me So Much Peace!

reviews.author: Chasity Fortham
reviews.text:
Kelli was absolutely amazing! The food and coffee were both delicious, and the atmosphere was beautiful, warm, and welcoming. I was even able to enjoy my Zoom meeting comfortably while having a great bite to eat.

As a tourist, I really appreciated Kelli taking the time to share local recommendations—her dinner spots were fire! Thank you, Kelli, for the great hospitality. Whenever I’m back in Atlanta, AEO 404 Coffee will definitely be my first stop! ☕✨

reviews.author: Gabriel Martinez
reviews.text: My wife and I had a great time here! We were nervous to try something new at first but 404 did not disappoint. Our favorite part about this spot was the food. It was super fresh and levels above your local Starbucks or dunkin in terms of quality and taste. The vibe is amazing and you learn a lot about Atlanta and what it has to offer through their city inspired decor. I’d definitely recommend to anyone visiting Atlanta.

reviews.author: Noel Heatherland
reviews.text: My partner and I love coming here. We like to support local owned business. The food is good and the drinks are really cool. There’s a lot of different options. I really like the lavender one and the golden one with turmeric. The staff are efficient and friendly and it’s always a good experience.

google_address: 400 Chattahoochee Row NW, Atlanta, GA 30318, USA

reviews.author: Brigitte Porzio
reviews.text: Absolutely loved everything about Your 3rd Spot. First off, the staff was so attentive and very friendly! Everyone we encountered was so nice. Then THE ATMOSPHERE oh my goodness, every deal is so well thought out and it feels very high end. The game selection is phenomenal and along with that they also offer darts, basketball, shuffleboard, and an assortment of other activities. The bowling area is gorgeous too. We had the 7 course taste and explore menu for 2 and the cocktail tree. Every course was AMAZING and so tasty, like the food here is SO good! I have to go back and try everything on the menu. They also have a wonderful outdoor area with a slide - SO FUN! Cannot wait to go back here! Perfect for date night or girls night!

reviews.author: Mike Hopkins
reviews.text:
What Dave & Busters wishes it could be

We came here for a friends wedding celebration. Before you enter, you go into a room where they explain the rules and open up this wild door into what it seems to be a giant garden full of retro arcade games, it almost felt like a cult experience never done anything like it.

The food was delicious mostly appetizers. You order several and share them as a table. The games were fun games. I played them in my childhood growing up, which is the target demographic.

It may sound strange, but it is also the coolest bathroom experiences in my life. Birdie sounds in the background, finding snoop dog in the pictures on the wall walls, being overall clean and just peaceful. I came back from the bathroom with a sucker and my entire table Thought that was hilarious lol

Really neat, very unique venue highly recommend for a late night fun

reviews.author: Christian Gold
reviews.text:
Atmosphere: Great. Games: Great. Food & Drinks: A joke.

We ordered the chicken and waffle sando’s and a mini-eggo and 2 small chicken nuggets were delivered. Price: $12 💀 We got 2 cocktails. They came out in stemmed shot-glasses essentially. Price: $22. What really stung was gratuity was automatically added - a 20% gratuity. So if the service sucked, tough. Our drinks were never delivered and were over 30 mins late.

reviews.author: Annika Marie Singh
reviews.text: This place is sooo cool. My husband and I went for date night it was only $100 for a 7 course meal, bowling and gaming for three hours. The food was so good and we had so much fun. It’s really cool that they have an iPad set up to take pictures that you can then send to your phone. This is a great place for a date night or even for a big group celebration.

reviews.author: Kemuel Santiago
reviews.text: My wife and I had an incredible time we got the date night package which had free 30 min bowling (because we made a reservation prior to arrival.) and a 7 course meal that their head chef picks personally and rotates between seasons. The place has a multitude of games, from physical games like corn hole or jinga and tetris, to strategy games like these shuffle board style games and shuffle board as well. to video games like the typical racing and arcade games to the old school retro arcade games to actual retro consoles like sega.They did have curling but personally wasn't really able to slide it all the way I think the floors did need to be rewaxed but other than that everything was great. The waitors and waitresses were all very informed about the menu and was able to articulate the dish that was brought before us in detail. Our servers had an amazing attitude and was so kind and hardworking and it really made our 7th anniversary special. We will def be coming back for dates nights. P.s I believe it is 21 and older establishment most of the time. Might have a time period for children though

google_address: 264 Memorial Dr SE, Atlanta, GA 30312, USA

reviews.author: Adore
reviews.text: We had Togo yesterday and it was a waste of time energy and money. The parking lot needs some TLC the service was bare minimum. The energy was extremely flat. We had two plates plus extra beef. The meat brisket and beef rib (4/10) was tough and unflavored. The Mac n cheese (2/10) it was dry and just not pleasant period , the candied yams (1/10) they were burnt and tasted like burnt brown sugar, the broccoli casserole (6/10) it was fair had a little more flavor than all the other dishes/sides. The cornbread (3/10) it was dry but sweet. Overall I wouldn’t recommend this place to anyone that loves bbq avoid wasting your money here. Now disregard this review if you like your food as I described above.

reviews.author: Nancy Jane
reviews.text:
DON’T GO HERE. Waste of my $36.17.
Sold me RAW chicken that’s still clucking.

The rice and beans are a high blood pressure gateway.
The cornbread tastes like dry, unflavored cornmeal.

reviews.author: chandra Persaud
reviews.text:
BBQ AND SMOKEHOUSE BEST JOINT IN ATLANTA!!!!
If your staying in the city and want that southern comfort BBQ,man this is the best joint.It was finger lickick good.
They have kids meal too
ITs Smoked to perfection 👌
Taste is out of this 😋  world 10/10
Owners Chris and her brother was very kind and friendly.
This place is a vibe
Its 35 years in business and has a history.
Plus a 3 min drive from Atlanta zoo.

reviews.author: Sunny Jean
reviews.text: Another IG post that made me want to try Daddy D’z… I have mixed feelings about this place but ultimately my money was definitely wasted. I spent nearly $100 on 3 plates (rib and chicken combos) to get old and I mean old as hell hard as hell chicken. Ribs not tender at all. I couldn’t chew any of the meat. I had to throw it away. The Mac and cheese is these big ol shells… not a good choice for Mac and cheese but was not good at all either. It was so bad I had to ask myself…there is no way when they put this plate together looked at the chicken and thought this was ok. I was so disappointed… the customer service was great tho… but I won’t be returning unfortunately and I wanted to love this place sooooo bad!!!

reviews.author: Noah Marietta-Perez
reviews.text:
Parents used to take me here as a kid every week and I came back to try it as an adult. The food tastes exactly the same.

Trashy place, delicious food, good service.
It's certainly an experience!

google_address: 2841 Greenbriar Pkwy SW SUITE X101, Atlanta, GA 30331, USA

reviews.author: E. J.
reviews.text: I placed two orders, whiting and 3 pieces of chicken which comes with fries and bread. The other order was a  3 pieces whiting combo.  The food was horrible.  None of the fish and chicken were seasoned. I think they rely on that powder to take the place of seasoning the food.  That powder stuff does not take the place of real seasoning.  You could smell the fish a mile a way. Not in a good way.  My suggestion for JJ's is start seasoning your food properly and stop thinking that the food is good because of that white powder. Let that powder be an addition to your food after you have seasoned it.  I won't be going back.

reviews.author: mia Dupree
reviews.text: Not a single sprinkle of seasoning on my food which is crazy cus that’s literally what JJ’s is known for. How the hell you forget that. No ranch seasoning and the chicken and fish itself barely seasoned. Overall disappointment I could’ve kept my money and fried chicken at home.

reviews.author: Roe Show
reviews.text: 🛑 🖐🏽Avoid this place if possible. I ordered a 10 piece dinner but I only received 10 pieces of chicken only with no sides.  I took it back and they took my takeout back and handed me another takeout with 6 pieces and fries so I took it back again and told them my first order was a 10 piece not a 6 piece so they took it back again and handed me the correct order this time.  I asked can I get some napkins and condiments they pointed to the table I proceeded to the table and there was three packages of mustard and that was all that was on the table.  I told them that nothing is on the table so he handed me two hot sauces and that was it. Please don’t waste your time because this place doesn’t value your black hard earned money.

reviews.author: Emii Galdamez
reviews.text: J & J fish and chicken is an amazing place to eat! The wings are always hot and tasty, and the staff is super friendly and fast. I buy from them and also work through DoorDash, and every experience has been great—my orders are always fresh and on time. Love this place!

reviews.author: Devonte Malone
reviews.text:
This is undoubtedly one of the best JJ's Fish locations I've ever visited. When I say fire I mean fireeeee 🔥🔥🔥. The place was clean, and the workers provided excellent customer service. They consistently cooked the fish and chicken fresh 🔥🔥🔥 and served each order hot. You even get to add your own lemon pepper.

This is my second visit; I was here yesterday and felt compelled to return today 😂. This has definitely become my new go-to JJ's in Atlanta. I really appreciate everyone here. Keep up the great work. Thank you 🙏🏾

reviews.author: AJ J
reviews.text: Sweet perfection in every bite! I tried a variety box of macarons from Ché René at the French Fair, and they were all incredible. Highly recommend

reviews.author: julia wilcox
reviews.text: CheRene Macarons have been our go-to treat to celebrate client birthdays and celebrations. Always high quality, consistent and easy to communicate with, CheRene is an absolute gem! I highly recommend them!

reviews.author: Candace Terrell
reviews.text: This was an amazing experience. Everything was 20 out of 10. Customer Service had great energy, was very helpful and accommodating, price point very affordable, turn around time was QUICK, presentation was beautiful, AND THEY TASTED AS GOOD AS THEY LOOKED!! As a person who orders custom Macarons often this was the most pleasant and professional transaction I've had yet!!

reviews.author: Melissa Holland
reviews.text: Let me start off by saying I do not normally write reviews but this one was work sharing. We always find a macaron shop whenever we are on vacation.  Hands down these were a 10!!!!!! ChéRené and Taylor were the sweetest.  Carmel and cinnamon roll were my personal favorites but I didn't taste a single one that wasn't amazing.

reviews.author: Nicole Walton
reviews.text: Che is an absolute gem! ✨ I recently ordered macarons from her for my wife's birthday, and they were hands-down the best we've ever had—melt-in-your-mouth perfection! You can truly taste the love, passion, and craftsmanship in every bite. Not only were the macarons stunning and delicious, but Che went above and beyond to make sure everything was perfect for our celebration. Her attention to detail, kindness, and dedication to her craft made the whole experience so special. We're incredibly grateful for her talent and can’t wait to order again. If you’re thinking about placing an order—do it! The world deserves to experience Che’s magic. 💕👏🏽

reviews.text: Did an online order and they telling me i can’t pick it up inside? The females in here are dancing, not even doin their job at all. It takes seconds to hand me a bag. They drive thru not even moving. Yall are beyond are unprofessional. Be better.

The drive thru wasn't moving as fast as it should have. Only 3 cars when I came in and the same car that as at the window when I left.  Needs improvement on service. Cashier young lady/man ask we so what you want ! I was shocked. Do better. Food was hot and out the grease I can say

reviews.author: CeeJay Castlin
reviews.text: I really wish they would expand the location, it's so small and compact on the inside that it gives off claustrophobic vibes in my opinion. Also the score was an 86 👀 I'm not sure if that's a good thing or their up for review soon but that's got to change because you should be in the mid to high 90's. But the chicken was really fresh and tasty, also the drive-through box has been broken for God knows how long 🤦🏾‍♂️

reviews.author: Jim Raynor
reviews.text: Greatly improved over the year.  Doesn't taste like eating cardboard and they are much faster.  Use to take 30min -40 minutes for drive through in 2024.  Now they are better staffed and have better cooks. Less than 10 minutes I'm drive through during lunch hour.

reviews.author: Miss Hughes
reviews.text: I went there b4 and the food was good. I got macaroni and cheese, collards, and smoked chicken. This time I got the same thing but also a cheeseburger egg roll for $5.50. Y'all, when I tell yall that egg roll was so small and tasted burnt! They ought to be ashamed to even put something out like this. The macaroni was not good this time either, it was dry as heck.

reviews.author: Lisa Edwards
reviews.text: Ive passed and stopped at backyard grill on many occasions especially as a Dasher well I finally decided to stop and grab a plate for myself I got the chicken tenders dipped in teriyaki with fries (not to mention very reasonable price ) LOVED IT & came back the next day ! chicken tenders were BUSSIN! not the frozen ones but fresh and good batter! Fries were fried to perfection!! All condiments in the order just all around great service very homey feel to it and great vibes and music if you'd like to dine! Fresh food great taste and best of all African American owned ! I LOVE to spend my money in the community! Stop by and give them a try! You won't be disappointed!

reviews.author: Tanisha Billups
reviews.text: I placed a delivery order for the ribeye med rare with grilled broccoli and yellow rice. The rice is really good and the broccoli isn’t bad but the steak came well well done. This is my 1st time trying this restaurant and I’m very disappointed. I waited over an hour.

reviews.author: Ms. Lovy
reviews.text: This entire experience was piss poor... and I hate to be so hard on a black owned business, but the truth must prevail! Ordered the lamb chop dinner with grilled broccoli and Mac and cheese. EVERYTHING was cold which is pretty insane to me having waited 30+ minutes for this food. The broccoli was mushy and overcooked. How can grilled broccoli be mushy? My conclusion either it isn't fresh broccoli being grilled and or it's being steamed or boiled because the texture made no sense whatsoever. The Mac and cheese was flavorless and while the lamb chops had decent seasoning (meaning salt and pepper) the proportions size was laughable ; however, let me reiterate the big disappointment is how everything was cold. The young lady with the glasses was not 100% pleasant. So for about $32 with tax it was not worth it and was truly comical. Hated that the long awaited experience was so poor!

reviews.author: Crystal “Crystal” B
reviews.text: Backyard BBQ!! Take them eggrolls off that menu ASAP! Why yall playing on my face with this itsy bitsy egg roll that’s GROSS and overpriced for $6! The ribtips was good tho.

reviews.author: Andrew Kearney
reviews.text: I’ve ordered from Pastry Depot a handful of times now and am always satisfied as a customer. The order is delivered quickly. They have great prices on all the top chocolate makers. Simply put, they make sourcing pastry supplies a breeze. And their customer service is top notch. You will not be disappointed if you choose them for your pastry needs.

reviews.author: Arecee
reviews.text:

reviews.author: Art by Delvon
reviews.text: I visited this coffee shop for the first time since they moved from Sylvan Road to Murphy Ave. in West End Atlanta. They're now inside of Create ATL, an eclectic, create co-working space for creative businesses in Atlanta. It's a very cool and cozy atmosphere for lounging, relaxing, and catching up on work. Whether you're looking for a good cup of coffee in the area or want to check out a dope event space, this location is certainly worth the visit. While you're there, grab a warm pastry and check out some of the interesting books on display. I love cold brewed coffee with a splash of oat milk, and this place did not disappoint. The barista was kind enough to offer any needed adjustments to my drink, but it was perfect the first time. Thank you! I'll see you guys soon for sure.

reviews.author: Sandra Neal
reviews.text:
Found my forever coffee shop.

Spent last week looking for a space for study, and was disappointed.

Then today, found this space, perfect for those times where you’re sick of working/studying from home.

Wonderful atmosphere, large, & open. Felt a connect with my Tia Magdalena, drinking my cafecito at Cafexito :)

reviews.author: rodney
reviews.text:
The owner and staff are very friendly, and speak both English and Spanish.

The cafe is very spacious and nicely  decorated.  While I personally don't drink coffee, my wife says it's great.

They also serve limited but delicious pastries, empanadas and other Mexican drinks and snacks.

I'm definitely going make this cafe a part of my regular rotation.

reviews.author: Samaria R
reviews.text: Made a quick stop since we were in the area and I am so glad we did. I had the Horchata Latte which was a perfect combination of both things I love! They had pastries and I was told they have churros and empanadas on the weekends and soon they would offer them everyday. If you are in the area, you must stop by and try them out.

reviews.text: Really cute vibe at their new location on Murphy Ave with an attached co-working space! I got the Iced Oat-chata Latte, absolutely delicious 😍.  I'll definitely be back to try some pastries and other breakfast goodies.

reviews.text: The best American deli on this side of Atlanta. The staff is amazing. It’s very clean. The wait time isn’t bad at all and the quality food score is 100.

reviews.author: Xavier K
reviews.text:
I find myself frequently choosing this fast food restaurant for my lunch, even though I don't typically dine at other fast-food establishments. Today, I opted for one of my favorite items, the Whiting fish sandwich, along with a 5-piece wing combo. The price was very reasonable, perhaps the lowest in town. What I appreciate about this place is the variety of fish-related menu options they offer, including fish salad, fish sandwich, and full fish meals. They have both Whiting and Tilapia on their menu, but I always go for Whiting due to its nutritional benefits.

The meal was delightful. The fish was fresh and perfectly seasoned, making each bite a flavorful experience. The wings, dipped in teriyaki sauce, were also excellent. Despite being a small neighborhood franchise, the restaurant maintains a high level of cleanliness. The owner and employees take great care in ensuring the place is tidy and inviting. I particularly enjoy the ambiance with nice music playing in the background and news displayed on a giant screen TV with subtitles, so it doesn't disrupt the pleasant atmosphere.

Overall, I highly recommend this fast-food spot for anyone craving delicious fish dishes with friendly service and a clean dining environment. It's a hidden gem in the neighborhood that consistently delivers on taste and quality.

reviews.author: jaida woodson
reviews.text: Food is good and fresh. Really good service and they are fast. Best american deli ive been to.

reviews.author: Miranda Johns-Green
reviews.text: American Deli is GOATED in my books! My favorites are the medium buffalo wings and the sweet chili. And definitely have to get my peach and lemonade mixed. 😮‍💨🤞🏽❤️

reviews.author: Alena B
reviews.text: Black Coffee Atlanta is such an aesthetically pleasing and creative place to be. They have really cute names for their coffee, espresso, & tea options. There’s also a small section of light bites to enjoy. Highly recommend the Cash Money Matcha!!!! The cinnamon & vanilla twist really set it off!!! They have wifi and lots of seating if you’re a remote worker or just want to hang out and catch a vibe!

reviews.text:

They’re super friendly, easy to talk to, and always check in to make sure I’m good. The shop is clean, smells great, and most importantly, I feel safe and comfortable being there.

This is definitely one of my favorite spots now. Highly recommend. 💛

reviews.author: Nicholas Miles
reviews.text:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Black Coffee Cafe is a Hidden Gem!

I absolutely love Black Coffee Cafe! From the moment I walked in, the warm and inviting atmosphere made me feel right at home. The coffee is delicious—rich, smooth, and crafted with care. But it’s more than just the amazing drinks; the vibe here is perfect. Whether you're looking to get some work done, catch up with friends, or just enjoy a quiet moment, this is the spot.

What really sets Black Coffee Cafe apart is the strong sense of community. You can tell this is a place where people connect, support each other, and celebrate Black excellence. Speaking of excellence—I was thrilled to discover Christopher’s Favorite Tea, a new Black-owned tea company, here! Their tea was so flavorful and refreshing, and I love that the cafe highlights other Black-owned businesses.

Supporting Black Coffee Cafe means supporting a business that pours love into every cup and into the community. Do yourself a favor and stop by—you won’t regret it! **10/10 recommend!** ☕💛

reviews.author: Dr. Xia de Puma
reviews.text:
Black Coffee Atlanta is a true neighborhood gem.

Ww were greeted with warm smiles at the counter. The space is bright, airy and perfectly decorated with that vibe that only Atlanta delivers. Great playlist. Lively without being loud. It's the kind of place you love to visit on those "work outside the office" days.

We ordered the Thug Passion tea and a matcha. Both slapped in the best way. Thug Passion is bold and fragrant, with a citrusy pop that finishes silky. Zero bitterness. Just pure, deliciousness. The matcha is yummy and vibrant with a completely fresh sweet flavor. Both can be served hot or cold.

Service is quick. Drinks come with the coolest personalized notes written on the cups. Plenty of seating and outlets. Perfect for a quick pick me up or a laptop session.

YOUILOVE

reviews.author: Leah Klinge
reviews.text:
No-go for us. While Terra Terroir carries a fine-dining price point, the quality of food, service, and atmosphere fell well short of expectations.

Some describe this place as charming, but we felt the opposite; the interiors seem tired and could use some polish and updates, and most table florals were wilted. The garden area has potential, but the mix of faux plants, dated light fixtures, and inexpensive tables gives it a less refined vibe.

Menu-wise, prices don’t align with plating, flavor, or overall experience. Even the wine list leans heavily toward entry-level bottles rather than thoughtful pairings.

On the positive side, the host greeted us warmly, and we were seated right away (only two other tables were occupied on a Thurs @ 6p). Unfortunately, service lagged after that. Our server was pleasant but offered little guidance when asked about menu highlights (“everything” was the answer), water refills were sparse, and empty plates lingered well into the next course until we finally stacked them ourselves.

Food notes:
•Scallop & corn fritters – 6/10 ($18). Moist and flavorful but over-fried; plating felt amateur for the price, and the sauce was thick and dull. Honestly, LaGarde & Confab down the street both do significantly better fritters for less ($11), with a better vibe
•Jumbo crab cake – 6/10 ($21). Nice flavor, but again, not worth the price. (Even Chops Lobster Bar charges just a few dollars more for a far superior experience.) The jicama slaw was fresh but overly soaked in sugar water
•Burger – 9/10. The highlight of the night!
•Salmon – 4/10. Overcooked, overly salty, served on plain couscous with the saltiest green beans I’ve ever had, all topped with a syrupy blueberry sauce.
•Grapefruit & pomegranate salad – 6/10. Overdressed, with too much sweetness from the pomegranate-honey vinaigrette; the grapefruit tasted canned. Grilled shrimp were cooked nicely.

Not sure how this earned an OpenTable Diners’ Choice 2025 badge… it wouldn’t crack my top 50 spots in Atlanta. And yes, a cat ran across the rafters mid-meal.

reviews.author: L Barnes
reviews.text:
The atmosphere was relaxing and enjoyable. The food was delicious as well. We had a last minute cancellation in another venue for a birthday dinner. I called them the day before and we were able to get a reservation for 16 people at the last minute.

When we arrived we were greeted and seated quickly. We waited on one guest then ordered. The bread came out quickly and so did the appetizers for the ones that ordered. The main courses did take a little longer to arrive which is understandable with a larger party.

Everyone enjoyed their food and most said it's a beautiful setting for a wedding dinner or a large party.

My lamb was cooked perfectly and I thoroughly enjoyed the jam and goat cheese sides. The French Country Dinner was another great meal. The duck was nice and crispy, the beans paired well with the cranberries and sausage.

I will be returning and bringing my family to enjoy Terra Terroir!

reviews.author: Brian Lawson
reviews.text:
Last night we dined at Terra Terroir, and overall, it was a fantastic experience. The food was flavorful, the ambiance was beautiful, and the service was excellent.

We started with the jumbo lump crab cakes, which were a hit—crispy on the outside, tender on the inside, and full of fresh crab flavor. The vanilla yams were an interesting twist—while the vanilla was a bit strong, they were still enjoyable. The gambas al ajero (garlic shrimp) were packed with flavor and perfectly cooked, definitely one of the standout appetizers. The lamb lollipop chops were well-seasoned and delicious, though on the smaller side in terms of portion.

For the main course, I had the slow-braised pork shank, which was hearty, tender, and full of flavor—a very satisfying dish with generous portions. We also ordered the Kansas City strip, and although I didn’t try it myself, my son’s friend commented on how flavorful and tender it was. The seafood special, featuring Alaskan halibut, was another hit at the table—well-prepared and enjoyed by everyone who tried it.

One note: several of us noticed that our dishes were not served hot, more lukewarm. While it didn’t detract significantly from the overall experience, it’s something worth mentioning.

The restaurant’s interior is beautifully decorated, evoking a European ambiance that makes you feel like you’ve stepped into a different part of the world. The service was attentive and warm from start to finish.

Final Verdict: Terra Terroir is definitely a spot worth visiting. If you're in town and looking for a memorable dining experience with great food and charming atmosphere, put this place on your list. Just hope the kitchen turns up the heat on those plates next time!

reviews.author: Ford Fincher
reviews.text:
My best friend, and dear coworker, passed away today and I wasn’t at work to grieve with my team so I thought I’d take them is favorite lemon layer cake that we always get him for his birthday. Unfortunately, our regular shop doesn’t carry it anymore, so I was on the hunt.
A few calls later, I found Sweet Cheats and their lemon cupcakes - but they only had 3. I needed 2 dozen. Without even hearing my sob story, they told me they would have my 2 dozen ready in 2 hours. Then they heard my story and I received their condolences- but the true customer service came without the hesitation to provide me what I needed in a timely manner. However, the quality was UNEXPECTEDLY DELIGHTFUL!!! Explosive flavor. Moist, yet dense, cake. And explosive color. Perry would have absolutely loved them. So I had a second one for him (at least that’s what I’m telling myself).

THANK YOU THANK YOU THANK YOU!!!
You brought a little sunshine the darkest day!!

reviews.author: Simon Rimmer
reviews.text: As a wedding DJ/ AV Provider, We love working at Guardian Works. The venue has amazing acoustics, a smooth load-in, and a staff that’s always organized and welcoming. Beautiful space, great energy, and always a fantastic place for events.

reviews.text: Went for the Atlanta food and wine festival. Beautiful venue.

reviews.author: Paul Stoddard
reviews.text: Lovely wedding/party venue situated in the heart of Atlanta, without feeling like you're stuck in the middle of a big city. Housed in a former perfume factory, it has an old warehouse charm with several well-appointed, intimate spaces. I had the pleasure of being a groomsman & catering a wedding here. The outdoor space is well covered and a couple hundred people could easily enjoy hanging out outside if it were raining & have no fear of getting wet. Check this place out for your next event for sure!

reviews.author: Tommy Nagle (Albion Reid Weddings)
reviews.text:
Guardian Works is industrial poetry in motion. I helped photographed Courtney and Aaron’s fall wedding here, and the venue’s gritty-meets-glitter(y) energy stole the show - think 24-foot exposed beams dripping with disco balls (their bold spin!) and original 1908 red brick walls that whispered stories of its past life as a chemical factory and perfumery. (Yes, they’ve kept oversized scales and vintage vats as quirky decor.) The floor-to-ceiling windows flooded the 6,000 SF main space with golden light, while the 10,000 SF fenced lawn (hello, ceremony potential!) felt like a secret garden tucked into West Midtown’s buzz.

What impressed me? Flexibility without fuss. Custom decor with zero pushback, and the seamless execution from the staff Logistically, it’s a photographer’s ally: tons of free parking (no valet scramble!), ADA access, loads of space. Capacity-wise, it’s ideal for 150–250 guests—spacious enough for a live band under the skylights but cozy for toasts in the pre-function area.

Pro tips for couples:

• Lean into the history: Use the original freight elevator (yes, it’s there!) as a photo op or quirky champagne nook.

• Outdoor magic: The lawn’s $750 ceremony add-on includes 200 white chairs—worth it for golden-hour vows.

• Catering savvy: Stick to their amazing approved list (like Fox Bros or Proof of the Pudding) to dodge the $2K outside fee.

If you want a venue that’s equal parts warehouse soul and downtown edge—with a team that lets your vision run wild -Guardian Works is it.   📸 IG @AlbionReid

reviews.author: Doc Mad
reviews.text:
I’ve always had a soft spot for Ethiopian food, and Desta reminded me exactly why. From the moment I walked in, the warm, aromatic notes of berbere and sautéed onions filled the air—an unmistakable welcome that tells you you’re in for something special.

The menu itself is thoughtfully designed, guiding you through the experience based on how adventurous you’re feeling. It’s the kind of place where first-timers can explore with confidence, and seasoned fans can still find something new to savor. I sampled a few dishes that beautifully balanced earthy lentils, spiced meats, and tangy injera—that soft, slightly sour flatbread that ties every bite together. Each dish felt like a conversation between flavor and culture.

Desta delivers not just a meal, but a moment—one that feels both global and familiar. And now that I’ve discovered there’s a Desta in Dallas, I can’t help but wonder if it shares the same spirit as the Atlanta location. Either way, I plan to find out firsthand, fork (or injera) in hand.

reviews.text: 10/10 recommend. The food was flavorful, tasty, fresh, and satisfying. It was all my vegan dishes on one plate. We weren't sure what everything was, so the waitress took the time to explain things to us. My go-to was the vegan platter. The mushrooms, lentils, chickpeas—everything was excellent! My husband had the grouper, and he said it reminded him of his childhood.
